title: test/system: fix quadlet - rootfs on kernel 7.1
body:
As found in fedora openQA testing on the kernel update this test fails due a selinux denial.

The kernel now correctly checks the backing fs selinux context in all cases. See kernel commit 82544d36b172 (selinux: fix overlayfs mmap() and mprotect() access checks).

The test tries to access another container so the selinux level will be different from the quadlet roofs container and therefore fail to access the files.

To fix this we should be able to just use the regular image mount which has a level all containers can access. The comment that this is not parallel safe is untrue, mounts are reference counted so this should not get unmounted all of the sudden by another test.

In fact we have another  --rootfs test case where we do this and it passed in openQA, see "podman run - check workdir".

Fixes: #28602

comments.body:
> I assume we must merge to see the updated OpenQA results; if so, LGTM.

openQA uses our packaged podma-tests rpm to run regression tests on most bohdi updates. So we need to not just merge but also package it into fedora, hence my 5.8 label so we get this into the stable fedoras as well.
You can reproduce easily by just using the 7.1 kernel build from rawhide and I did confirm this works on my local VM.

For now the openQA workaround is to skip the test which we have to do from time to time, https://forge.fedoraproject.org/quality/os-autoinst-distri-fedora/commit/4e94e5ef66ee88f495203a48eeffc68e8c9e3484

And since that skip unblocked the kernel from going into rawhide we now enjoy that failure on packit rawhide tests:

https://artifacts.dev.testing-farm.io/672d3cbd-5e9f-4b90-80fe-5962f3354c83/

So yeah please lets get this in to not get annoying packit comment son each PR even if we do not block on them.
